scenarios.title: Data Quality Validation
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when a task mentions Great Expectations, GX Core, data quality checks, expectation suites, validation definitions, checkpoints, Data Docs, or unexpected rows.
scenarios.role: Guides coding agents through current GX Core Python APIs for context setup, data connection, expectation suites, validations, checkpoints, actions, and Data Docs.
scenarios.read_when: great_expectations, GX Core, gx.get_context, ExpectationSuite, ValidationDefinition, Checkpoint, Data Docs, datasource, data asset, batch definition, expectation, validation result, unexpected rows
scenarios.best_for: Building or debugging local GX Core workflows with Python APIs, pandas/filesystem/SQL assets, expectation suites, validation runs, result-format choices, checkpoints, and safe notification/Data Docs configuration.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is exclusively about GX Cloud UI operations, unrelated data validation frameworks, contributed third-party expectation packages, or backend service administration rather than GX Core code.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `great-expectations` for Great Expectations or GX Core coding tasks. Start at the root route map, then read only the sub-skill matching the current layer: context, data connection, expectations, validation results, or checkpoint actions.

scenarios.title: Python Package Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when a Python task asks to inspect or use the installed great_expectations package APIs rather than editing Great Expectations internals.
scenarios.role: Provides installed-package API facts, safe smoke helpers, and troubleshooting for Great Expectations as a Python dependency.
scenarios.read_when: Python import great_expectations, package API inspection, public API signatures, no CLI entry point, optional dependencies, pydantic validation errors
scenarios.best_for: Writing scripts or tests that import GX, instantiate public GX objects, and verify behavior with tiny local fixtures without relying on original repository files.
scenarios.avoid_when: The user is asking for maintainer-only repository release, docs build, CI, lint, or contribution workflow automation.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Use this scenario when the task is about consuming Great Expectations from another project or verifying available public APIs; route maintainer internals elsewhere.
